notes: Chronological CV concern — reviewer's concern is backwards on all runs across all conditions. Critic and Defender both independently identified reviewer error in isolated debate. Key disclosure item across runs: COVID window sensitivity, fold-level AUC stability reporting.

Threshold manipulation concern — 0.35 threshold is mathematically correct from 4.2:1 cost ratio, pre-specified one month before data analysis. All conditions unanimous. Key disclosure items: cost ratio derivation documentation, single-site generalizability, threshold sensitivity table.

AUPRC cherry-picking concern — reviewer has direction of cherry-picking backwards (AUPRC=0.61 < AUC-ROC=0.963). Pre-specified six weeks before training. Both metrics reported. All conditions unanimous. Key disclosure: AUPRC prevalence-dependence for benchmarking, deployment threshold metrics.

Logistic regression vs XGBoost — reviewer's performance argument fails given binding UDAAP and model risk management policy constraints. Pre-specified power analysis confirms AUC>=0.75 achievable, model achieves 0.779. All conditions unanimous. Multiround run 2 noted train/test split documentation gap.

Hardware heterogeneity confounding concern — deliberate domain generalization strategy validated by pre-analysis ablation (0.944 vs 0.887 AUC cross-site). All conditions unanimous. Multiround run 1 surfaced test set composition clarification (within-distribution vs cross-site labeling).

Catalog expansion regime change concern — team proactively tested pre/post nDCG@10 and found 0.02 difference, documented decision. Regime change concern is empirically dismissed. All conditions unanimous. Multiround runs surfaced cold-start handling and statistical characterization of 0.02 difference.

Loan officer tenure proxy discrimination concern — correctly handled by pre-deployment disparate impact analysis with legal counsel, no significant impact found. Feature removal not warranted. All conditions unanimous. Multiround run 3 raised output-level vs feature-level analysis distinction as a process completeness item.

Batch 5 complete. All 7 defense_wins cases produced unanimous defense_wins verdicts across all 4 conditions and 3 runs each. No contamination events. Multiround condition did not bias toward critic framing — Defender seeing Critique did not cause verdict drift toward critique_wins in any of the 21 multiround runs. ETD produced in 2 ensemble runs (009 runs 1 and 3) where output-level disparate impact analysis was specified as a required supplementary test.
